PRIVACY FIRST

Federated
Learning

Train models collaboratively without sharing raw data. Your data stays local — only encrypted model updates travel across the network.

The Privacy Promise

Data Never Leaves

Raw training data stays on the provider's device. Only model gradients are shared.

Differential Privacy

Mathematical guarantees ensure no individual data point can be reconstructed from updates.

Encrypted Updates

All model updates are encrypted end-to-end. Only the aggregation result is decrypted.

The Federated Cycle

1

Initialize Global Model

A base model is created and distributed to all participating providers. The model architecture is public; weights are initialized randomly.

2

Local Training

Each provider trains the model on their local data for N epochs. The raw data never leaves their device.

3

Gradient Extraction

After local training, providers compute model weight updates (gradients). These are compressed and encrypted.

4

Secure Aggregation

Encrypted gradients are sent to an aggregation server. The server combines updates without seeing individual contributions.

5

Model Update

The aggregated update is applied to the global model. A new round begins with the updated weights.

Security Comparison

ApproachData SharingPrivacy RiskUse Case
CentralizedAll data to central serverHigh — single breach exposes everythingSingle organization, trusted environment
DistributedData splits across nodesMedium — partial exposure possibleMulti-organization, controlled access
Federated (DICOMPUTE)Only gradients sharedLow — differential privacy guaranteesCross-organization, privacy-critical data

Ready to Train Privately?

Start a federated learning job today. Your data stays on your device, while the global model improves from collective intelligence.

DICOMPUTE — VERIFIABLE COMPUTE ON XDC APOTHEM
